entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A valuable oil-seed crop in Tanzania characterized by labor-intensive production.

entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: The country where the study on groundnut production and labor-saving technologies is conducted.

entities.name: LABOR-SAVING TECHNOLOGIES
entities.description: Technologies introduced to reduce the manual labor required for groundnut production.

entities.name: LABOR INTENSITY
entities.description: The high amount of manual effort required for various activities in groundnut production.

entities.name: PLOUGHING
entities.description: A labor-intensive activity in groundnut production involving soil preparation.

entities.name: PLANTING
entities.description: A labor-intensive activity in groundnut production involving sowing seeds.

entities.name: WEEDING
entities.description: A labor-intensive activity in groundnut production involving the removal of weeds.

entities.name: HARVESTING
entities.description: A labor-intensive activity in groundnut production involving gathering the crop.

entities.name: SHELLING
entities.description: A labor-intensive activity in groundnut production involving removing seeds from pods.

entities.name: TRADITIONAL TOOLS
entities.description: Conventional tools used by farmers that are compared against labor-saving technologies.

entities.name: FARMER PERCEPTION
entities.description: The attitudes and views of farmers regarding labor-saving technologies in groundnut production.

entities.name: POLICY ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: The framework of policies in Tanzania that affect the adoption of labor-saving technologies.

entities.name: RESOURCES
entities.description: Inputs such as time and finances used by farmers in groundnut production.
